title: Need help resolving SSR missmatch bug
body:
EDIT:

I (kind of) resolved the issue by rewriting some stuff so that Events get auto-hidden in SSG, which gets revalidated hourly. Thus the amount of elements in the DOM is now equal between server and client.

I also found and fixed another missmatch of `document.body`'s `class` attribute by splitting up a `useEffect` with an inbetween state. This is supposed to pick a random background from `/assets/backgrounds/landing/` with the list of files being passed by `getStaticProps` under the `backgrounds` prop.

https://github.com/Mitsunee/fgo-tools/blob/e41298b4243feed8421ec5f31ec64c21f58b5b91/pages/index.jsx#L27-L50

Is this the only way to get around SSR missmatches on the first render? This would cause the site to display older data until the second render completes, which I would prefer to avoid as it looks rather jumpy. I also want to avoid this for subjects of the "Display Spoilers" setting, as this could possibly display things the user wishes to have censored to avoid spoiling future game content.

-------

**Original Post**:

I've been banging my head against this for 3 days now...

I have a nextjs app that shows timers for the events in the game Fate/Grand Order. It's currently all done through static generation which seems to be biting me in the leg here. I have a list of events on the (currently rather bloated...) frontpage that I would like to be at least functional for navigation without javascript enabled.

The problem I am now having is that events ending (and thus getting hidden from the list) causes an SSR missmatch which causes a bug where some of the events use the wrong image and `href` attribute, but the correct title and onClick event.

I have documented the issue further in my repository: https://github.com/Mitsunee/fgo-tools/issues/54 and reproduced the bug in a separate branch: https://github.com/Mitsunee/fgo-tools/commits/reproduce-homepage-bug

What can I do resolve this missmatch, while retaining navigation with javascript disabled? So far I have been wrapping all timers and things affected by settings (stored in `localStorage`) in my `<NoSSR>` component, but that solution does not help here as I want to actually have the links statically generated at build time.
author: Mitsunee

title: Webpack asset/source returns JSON as a string
body:
Hey All,

Looking for some help to understand what is going on here.

**Test Repo**
https://github.com/lukehillonline/nextjs-json-demo
NextJs 12
Webpack 5
SSR

**Steps To Reproduce**
1. Download the test repo and install packages
2. Run `yarn build` and wait for it to complete
3. Open `/.next/server/pages/index.js` to see the SSR page
4. On line 62 you'll find the JSON object as a string
5. Open `.next/static/chunks/pages/index-{HASH}.js` to see the Client Side page
6. If you format the code you'll find the JSON object as a string on line 39

**The Problem**
We are using a translation service that requires creating JSON resource files of copy, and within these resource files, we need to add some specific keys that the service understands so it knows what should and should not be translated.

To do this as simple as possible I want to import JSON files into my code without them being tree shaken and minified. I just need the plain JSON file included in my bundle as a JSON object.

**The Solution - or so I thought**
The developers at the translation service have instructed me to create a webpack rule with a type of `assets/source` to prevent tree shaking and modification.

This _almost_ works but the strange thing is that the JSON gets added to the bundle as a string like so
```
module.exports = "{\n  \"sl_translate\": \"sl_all\",\n  \"title\": \"Page Title\",\n  \"subtitle\": \"Page Subtitle\"\n}\n";
```

This of course means that when I try and reference the JSON values in my JSX it fails.

**Help!**
If anyone can help me understand what is going wrong or how I can improve the webpack rule to return a JSON object rather than a string that would be a massive help.

Cheers!

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

Windows

### How are you deploying your application?

Vercel

### Describe the Bug

Since Next.js `11.1.3-canary.69`, ESM externals has been turned on by default using the `experimental: { esmExternals: true }` in this [commit](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/pull/29878).

Unfortunately ESM external is still not compatible with a lot of packages and the only way to turn it off is by setting the following config:

`experimental: { esmExternals: false }`

The problem is that any configuration set under `experimental` triggers the following warning logs, which for this case seems misleading:

```sh
warn  - You have enabled experimental feature(s).
warn  - Experimental features are not covered by semver, and may cause unexpected or broken application behavior. Use them at your own risk.
```

This is related to https://github.com/vercel/next.js/discussions/33148

### Expected Behavior

I would expect that `esmExternals` is moved from `experimental` to "top-level" and stops triggering the warning.

### To Reproduce

Simply set `experimental: { esmExternals: false }` in `next.config.js`

title: Disable Irrelevant Warnings and Output
body:
### Describe the feature you'd like to request

```
API response for /file exceeds 4MB. This will cause the request to fail in a future version. 
https://nextjs.org/docs/messages/api-routes-body-size-limit
```

- I am not utilizing serverless functions.
- I do not plan on utilizing serverless functions.
- I do not plan on utilizing my project for more than a dozen users.
- I am utilizing an external resolver for said API route.

Thus, this response size limit does not apply to me.

Utilizing a CDN is overkill for my project. I do not want to serve my files with a CDN for such an application, especially since it incurs cost.

This warning is very helpful, but it is in no way applicable to my project or how I plan to use it. It's uselessly cluttering up my output.

Can I turn this warning off? Also, will Next.js eventually break my API __even when its run in an environment__ where the 4 MB size limit does not apply? That would be **very** annoying.

### Describe the solution you'd like

Additional configuration could be added to `next.config.js`. 

Any non-error messages should reference an `outputConfig` entry and allow some outputs to be turned off.

```ts
import getConfig from 'next/config';

if (getConfig().outputConfig['api-routes-body-size-limit'] != false)
    console.log('API response for /file exceeds 4MB...');
```

`next.config.mjs`
```ts
// Used to toggle various behaviors for Next.js's output.
export const outputConfig = {
    // false = "silence" / dont show
    "api-routes-body-size-limit": false,
    // names should correspond to links shown in the output so its easy to keep track of them.
}
```

Again, this warning is not applicable to me and I just want to be able to turn it off.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

- Scroll more than I need to because my output is obscured by multiple instances of said warning.
- Reinvent the wheel and write an encapsulating program to filter it out of the output (not worth the effort)
author: cseitz

title: Cannot A/B test static pages using middleware
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

_No response_

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.7

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.13.1

### What browser are you using?

Edge (Chromium)

### What operating system are you using?

Windows 10

### How are you deploying your application?

Azure App Service

### Describe the Bug

Relevant information specifically for this bug: We host Next.js in Azure on our own in Azure.

We tried to run an A/B experiment using the new middleware functionality on two **static** pages: index.tsx and index2.tsx.

The problem we are having is that for static pages Next.js emits a cache control header that makes our CDN cache one page or another. And we cannot override cache control headers (to set it to no-cache) for static pages in next.config.js. Here's what the [docs](https://nextjs.org/docs/going-to-production#caching) say: Cache-Control headers set in next.config.js will be overwritten in production to ensure that static assets can be cached effectively.

### Expected Behavior

- Do not emit cache control headers that cache a page when it is served through a rewrite from middleware.
- OR: Allow overriding Cache-Control header for static pages in next.config.js, at least if middleware is involved in rewriting them.

### To Reproduce

Create two static pages, have a _middleware randomly choose between them. Notice if you put a CDN in front of the website that A/B testing will fail to work as expected because one of the sides (A or B) will get cached by the CDN.

Is there a good way to create reusable components with options to alter what they display from the API?

title: Client side data preparation
body:
What's the best way to prime client-side data loading in nextjs? I'm pretty excited by React 18's concurrent rendering and suspense. A big part of this is data loading with suspense, and starting your data loading before rendering. For example, with relay calling loadQuery on transition to a page instead of during its first render, then using usePreloadedQuery in the render. However, I couldn't find a good place to call loadQuery with next.js. If there was a simple getClientsideProps called before page renders I could use that (or some kind of router hooks that could be integrated nicely with page metadata).

An ideal setup would be for the router to prioritize loading this data preparation function and call that right away, then worry about getting the components downloaded after.

Has anyone else found a solution to this? Is this something nextjs will address as Suspense / concurrent mode get more adopted?

title: Allow jsconfig-path-plugin to supports specified packages in node_modules
body:
### Describe the feature you'd like to request

Currently, my team is using [next-transpile-modules](https://github.com/martpie/next-transpile-modules) to "inject" a package (our UI library) into next.js application. We're using [linaria](https://github.com/callstack/linaria) as styled engine and to be able to do theming, we're using [babel-plugin-module-resolver](https://github.com/tleunen/babel-plugin-module-resolver) to "point to" the correct theme that we want. This approach works.

However, if the built-in `jsconfig-path-plugin` can do the similar task, then we don't need to use the `babel-plugin-module-resolver` anymore. 



### Describe the solution you'd like

Instead of using fixed [NODE_MODULES_REGEX](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/canary/packages/next/build/webpack/plugins/jsconfig-paths-plugin.ts#L202), can you allow us passing in an option object to control this condition.

author: Nitin2108

title: Next.js scalable architecture template
body:
Hi, i recently made this Next.js [template](https://github.com/shreyas-jadhav/next-architecture-template) made with **`next-auth`, Material UI, mongodb nodejs driver, Joi Validation & `react-hook-from`**. It has everything set up in place to get started. 

**Server** and **Client** folders contains the separated code.. further divided in controllers, dals (data access layers) which can allow easily switching databases. Server has controllers made in such way to easily switch to express when needed. And there is **automatic error handling** in APIs and also client.

**models** folder which can contain all typescript interfaces and Joi schemas which will validate in both server side and client side. `react-hook-form` blends well with joi schemas and super useful to avoid writing lot of code.

`next-auth` is already setup.. you can plug your own providers and configuration, and basics work. 

There are also helper components and middlwares already built, like `withAuth` (sends 403) and `AuthRequried` (shows login if not auth)  which can be used to wrap controllers / components.

There are lot of things to know more about, I recommend reading here [https://next-architecture-docs.vercel.app/](https://next-architecture-docs.vercel.app/).

or you can checkout the code itself: [https://github.com/shreyas-jadhav/next-architecture-template](https://github.com/shreyas-jadhav/next-architecture-template)

title: Out of the box https solution for local development
body:
Devtools like vite or create-react-app have just simple solution to serve the dev server over https. This is still unsecure I know, but it is possible to create simple local certs to make https secure possible.

I couldn't find an easy solution to just start a https server with nextjs, I found out that other devtools do that a lot easier.

In vite you can start a https server like this:
```sh
vite --https
```

In create react app like this:
```sh
HTTPS=true react-scripts start
```

### Why do I need https for local development
I was building app with nextjs that included the MediaDevices api, but you can only use the api if you're running on https. Ended up using ngrok, what was ok for that project.
And I think there are a lot more reasons why running a devserver over https is usefull.

But why is their no out of the box https solution?

author: italodeandra

title: Fast Refresh support with preserved local state for coffeescript
body:
### Describe the feature you'd like to request

You can add a `coffee-loader` to the `webpack` part of `next.config.js` and Fast refresh works okey, but local state inside components are lost. It would be nice if the local state could be kept.

I realise this will be so down prioritised, it would never happen, but could you give me a tip for how to implement it myself? See the technical details of the problem below.

### Describe the solution you'd like

Local state inside components being kept in coffeescript file during Fast Refresh.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

Given a basic setup like so:

	config.module.rules.push({
		test: /\.(coffee)$/,
		include: [dir],
		exclude: /node_modules/,
		use: [
			defaultLoaders.babel,
			{
				loader: 'coffee-loader',
				options: {
					bare: true,
				}
			},
		],
	})

coffee-loader will compile to "fancy" js and `defaultLoaders.babel` will compile to "basic" js + **TRY TO** add the fast refresh stuff like

```
var _c, _c1;
$RefreshReg$(_c, "Home");
$RefreshReg$(_c1, "Hello");
```

But it will fail, and it seems it's because it's looking for a pattern of a functional component like so:
```
var Hello = function() {...
```
and coffeescript moves all `var`s to the top of the file while compiling like so:

```
var Hello, OtherComponent;
Hello = function Hello() { ...
```
and thus the pattern is not matched.

If we manually "improve" the output from the coffeescript compiler (before piping it in to the defaultLoaders.babel loader) by adding back the `var` like so :
```
var OtherComponent;
var Hello = function() {...
```
the `$RefreshReg$(_c1, "Hello");` gets added and Fast Refresh works with preserving the local state.

Is there any way to configure the `defaultLoaders.babel` to recognise patterns without the `var` as a component in order to add the  `$RefreshReg$(_c1, "Hello");` ?

title: Scroll restoration after page refresh with scrollRestoration experiment
body:
Hi everyone,

I recently enabled `scrollRestoration` experiment in our project to support SPA navigation scroll restoration, but I found that after adding this feature, the scroll position is not maintained after page refresh.

After some investigation, I found the issue is when we have `scrollRestoration: true`, `window.history.scrollRestoration` is set to `manual`. However, to enable scroll restoration after page refresh, we need `window.history.scrollRestoration` is set to `auto`. Seems like these two cases are conflicting with each other

Does anyone have the same problem or have any suggestion? 

Thanks in advance!!  😄

title: Does Next.js support custom entries in the package.json exports field?
body:
**Question**: Does Next.js support custom entries in the package.json exports field?

**Usecase**:
In a monorepo that contains a component package, I've got some custom entries defined for various tools and environments. So far every tool respected the custom entires and everything worked smoothly. But in the case of Next.js I'm not able to force it to use the custom `next` entry, it always tries to read the `"."` or `".": { "import": "..." }` which in my case ends up in an error message `Module not found: Package path . is not exported from package ...`.

**Solutions tried:**
- Adding a `resolve.conditionNames = ['next']` to the weback configuration in `next.config.js` _[1]_
- Adding node options to the development script `"dev": "NODE_OPTIONS='--conditions=next' next dev"` _[2]_
- Trying both at the same time

_[1] https://webpack.js.org/configuration/resolve/#resolveconditionnames_
_[2] https://nodejs.org/api/packages.html#resolving-user-conditions_

**Example**:
_Exports field defined in component package.json_
```json
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "storybook": "./src/index.ts",
      "next": "./dist/index.js",
    }
  }
```

author: LuckeeDev

title: Customization options for screen-reader announcement on route-change
body:
### Describe the feature you'd like to request

[Currently](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/3ceb9c567339e50fc6326a228841615a9a9edeac/packages/next/client/route-announcer.tsx#L12-L27), the `document.title` is given highest priority for screen-reader announcement upon route-change. That's reasonable, but it would be better for accessibility to give even higher priority to something else (something opt-in), so that developers are able to decouple the `document.title` from what screen-readers announce.

The reason (or _a_ reason, anyway) is that it's a common pattern to have the `document.title` include the site name, and if the site name is long then announcing it in full with every route-change is likely to annoy people using screen-readers. Think of an organization with a long official name and a shorter version that's typically used in communications—might be a good choice to use the full official name in the `document.title` but have screen-readers announce the short version instead.

### Describe the solution you'd like

Perhaps _highest_ priority could be given to an optional `data-sr-title` attribute on the `<title>` tag, and then the `document.title` could be used as the first back-up. Within a [`<Head>`](https://nextjs.org/docs/api-reference/next/head) component, then, one might have:

```js
<title
  data-sr-title="Page Title | Short Site Title"
>Page Title | Full Site Title Is Long and We Don't Want to Force Screen-Readers to Announce the Whole Thing</title>
```

### Describe alternatives you've considered

Maybe the `<Head>` component could accept a prop for this purpose instead.

author: drscottlobo

title: next/image should support running behind a proxy
body:
### Describe the feature you'd like to request

With the recent introduction of HTTP agent config through the very beneficial `keepAlive` (https://nextjs.org/docs/api-reference/next.config.js/disabling-http-keep-alive) being enabled by default Next.js is starting to become opinionated about how fetch agents should work as a default.

With this in mind I think it would also be a good time to **introduce proxy configuration options to allow `next/image` (and other future framework layer HTTP requests) to be ran from behind a proxy.**

The primary use-case is for applications forced to run behind an egress proxy to validate outgoing requests.

Issues/discussions describing the same problem:
- https://github.com/vercel/next.js/issues/20837
- https://github.com/vercel/next.js/discussions/18376
- https://github.com/vercel/next.js/discussions/21874

### Describe the solution you'd like

## Solution 1 (Preferred)

Use the widely accepted HTTP_PROXY and HTTPS_PROXY variables when configuring the fetch agent.

## Solution 2

An extension of the existing `httpAgentOptions` to include a proxy configuration, e.g in `next.config.js`

```js
module.exports = {
  httpAgentOptions: {
    keepAlive: false,
    proxy: {
        protocol: 'https',
        host: 'https://my-proxy-address.com',
        port: 443
  },
}
```

### Describe alternatives you've considered

An alternative solution is [to override the HTTP agent globally like described here](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/issues/20837) but with this solution it will also [overwrite the keep alive that the Next.js framework introduces with great benefit](https://github.com/gajus/global-agent#what-is-the-reason-global-agent-overrides-explicitly-configured-https-agent).
Without access to the framework I don't see how we can merge agent configs on a per-option basis in a good way, hence the global overwriting, resulting in overriding beneficial behaviours.
author: kungozan

title: How do you keep Vercel’s "Execution" usage under control?
body:
Hi everyone 👋🏻 

Sorry this is going to be a long text, hope some people will find the will to read it all and help us 🙏🏻 

We have recently launched a website for an online magazine client that gets a few visitors (15/30 at any given time, peaks at 80) and got a few bad surprises with our data usage on our Pro team plan.

Since it's a news website, a lot of navigation items and listings on the homepage caused us a lot of Execution time for a couple days before it caught our attention.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7307687/151945955-a587c78f-6257-4b55-9cf6-69a1442dd1b3.png)

Since then, we have been forced to do a few things in order to try to keep things under control:

1. Swap hyperlinks to `<Link prefetch={false} />` so it doesn't try to revalidate everything just by scrolling through the homepage
2. We initially had [SWR](https://swr.vercel.app/) on every page so it would refresh the content on the client-side if the revalidate was still waiting to be renewed. We were trying to be technically fancy but that's entirely removed now.
3. Increase our `revalidate` to `360` (is that enough? How do you guys set that value at for a website that has a lot of dynamic content?)
4. Since it's a heavy website on images too, we swapped our images to `<Image unoptimized={true} />` because we actually didn't really need it since images were optimized at the source (headless WordPress). I did not find any confirmation anywhere but is it wrong to believe that Next's image component uses serverless functions to optimize the images? Hence disabling it would positively affect the Execution time?

Since then (we have yet to see the impact of point 4), it's a lot better but even then we still seem to get between 30 and 50gb on a daily basis which by itself is too much for a single project (nearing 1TB monthly by itself alone… we have other client projects on our account!).

We were considering these last few options (please tell me if there are more available):

1. Reducing the memory allocated to functions by half [as underlined here](https://vercel.com/docs/cli#project-configuration/functions). Would that be a bad idea? Would all my functions timeout from the moment it gets lowered?
2. Find another hosting provider as Vercel's hosting choices between pro and enterprise is too steep for us. Maybe Netlify? Their pricing plan seems to state that "Serverless functions" are unlimited with their Business account. Would "Execution" from Vercel enter in that category?

We know there's an Enterprise plan but the price change is way too steep for us, starting at 36k/yearly apparently.
We have also contacted Vercel's support but unfortunately it does not seem that purchasing "packs" of 1TB/h is possible under Pro accounts, only Enterprise.

For guys that have websites with high affluence that need regular updates, what are your tricks to reduce the usage?
Do you just accept the fact to have revalidates really high and educate your client about how to live with it?

Unfortunately it seems that Vercel has been working on an API to flush specific pages built with ISR but [this discussion seems a bit stale for almost a year](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/discussions/11552#discussioncomment-646963)…

Thank you for reading!

title: Add DOM attribute when next/image is done loading
body: ### Describe the feature you'd like to request Hi there! I run a screenshot testing service called happo.io (proudly run on next.js since 2017). Lately I've had to debug a few issues with spurious screenshots coming from images constructed via `next/image`, for [Cypress test runs](https://docs.happo.io/docs/cypress). For screenshot testing to be stable, it's important that we know when images are fully loaded. With `next/image`, it's hard to do that without either: - inspecting the style attribute to look for `/filter.*blur/ - add a `load` event listener for the image element in user land - poll images for the `complete` property to be `true` To be clear, this is mostly a Cypress/e2e testing issue where Happo (and other services, like Percy) take a DOM snapshot of the page/element before sending that DOM snapshot off to a screenshot service. We need to know that the DOM is fully ready. We could add some code that will always wait for images to be downloaded before taking the DOM snapshot, but that would make a number of test suites slower, and we don't want that. We just need the `` element to be populated with enough information so that we can recreate it later. In the case of `next/image`, we need to wait for the DOM to be in the "ready" state -- no blurry background image. ### Describe the solution you'd like It would be super helpful if the underlying `img` element got a special attribute/class name when we know it's fully loaded. Something like ```html ``` Or perhaps the image can be in different states: ```html ``` ### Describe alternatives you've considered This is possible to solve in user land by adding something like ```js cy.get('img').should($imgs => $imgs.map((_i, img) => expect(img.complete).to.be.true) ) ```

title: No access to server-side primitives in _middleware.ts? Alternatives?
body:
Hi there. I'm looking at a project that uses a custom server (boo! hiss) and I'm exploring migrating to Next's middleware instead.

I've found that I can do most things (header mangling, redirects, `/api`-short-circuiting `nextUrl.pathname` behavior), but an obvious case that I would have expected to work out of the box is....writing to a file. Let's say I wanted to return the (`Next`)`Response` generated in the middleware interaction:

```ts
import fs from 'fs';

/* Request handling; response preparation */

fs.writeFileSync('/tmp/razzle.txt', Object.keys(preparedResponse).join('*'));

```

`Error: Module not found: Can't resolve 'fs'`

I *think* I understand what's going on here, 'fs' is not available to client-side code.

> The Edge Runtime has some restrictions including:
> 
> Native Node.js APIs are not supported. For example, you can't read or write to the filesystem

-- https://nextjs.org/docs/api-reference/edge-runtime#unsupported-apis

OK, I get that, but a middleware isn't _exactly_ a client-side thing. At the basis, I would expect that a middleware provides me access to the request, the response, and whatever other APIs I choose to use. Consider a canonical middleware use case: logging request paths and responses. If I don't have access to `fs`, `net.Socket`, etc. , how could that ever be implemented?

Searching the vercel/examples, there was no example. Anyone have a pattern on this?

author: Nutlope

title: Pattern to split Next.js app into modules/domain contexts
body:
## Problem

Hello, I'd like to be able to split my source code into modules/domain contexts, like so:

```
src/
├── auth/
│   ├── api/
│   │   ├── login.ts
│   │   ├── logout.ts
│   │   └── signup.ts
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── LoginForm.tsx
│   │   └── SignupForm.tsx
│   └── pages/
│       ├── login.tsx
│       └── signup.tsx
├── core/
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── Form.tsx
│   │   └── LabeledTextField.tsx
│   ├── layouts/
│   |   └── DefaultLayout.tsx
|   └── pages/
│       ├── _app.tsx
│       ├── _document.tsx
│       ├── 404.tsx
│       └── index.tsx
├── posts/
│   ├── api/
│   │   ├── createPost.ts
│   │   ├── getPost.ts
│   │   └── getPosts.ts
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── Post.tsx
│   │   └── Posts.tsx
│   └── pages/
│       └── posts/
│           ├── index.tsx
│           └── [slug].tsx
└── users/
    ├── api/
    │   └── getUser.ts
    ├── components/
    │   └── User.tsx
    └── pages/
        └── users/
            └── [id].tsx
```

Is there any known pattern for accomplishing something like this?

This is purely an organizational change; the application would still be built as one cohesive unit. In my experience, this kind of organization is necessary once your codebase grows above ~50k lines of code.

The Next.js automatic file-based routing would still work as expected, combining `pages/` and `api/` for all modules, and it would be up to the user to ensure that there are no conflicts (i.e. two different modules defining the same page route).

## Examples

[Blitz.js](https://blitzjs.com/) (pre framework-agnostic pivot) supports this [by default](https://blitzjs.com/docs/file-structure).

> The file structure nested inside `app/` can be anything you want, but we recommend the following:
>   - First layer folders to be a "domain context", like `core/`, `projects/`, `users/`, `billing/`, etc.
>   - All other folders like `components/` and `hooks/` go inside one of the above domain context folders

## Existing issues/discussions

I couldn't find much in Next.js issues or discussions that addresses this. https://github.com/vercel/next.js/issues/6040 is related, but focuses on complete build separation rather than simple organizational changes:

> Each part should be deployable separately

## Explored Solutions

The article [Dividing Your App Into Modules](https://www.parthkabra.me/nextjs/custom-routing) describes the issue well, and explores a few options:

1. [Custom Page Extensions](https://nextjs.org/docs/api-reference/next.config.js/custom-page-extensions) + [Rewrites](https://nextjs.org/docs/api-reference/next.config.js/rewrites) + importing/exporting
   This works, but it's very tedious to maintain the original structure in `pages/` and `api/` for the importing.
2. [Multi Zones](https://nextjs.org/docs/advanced-features/multi-zones)
   Much like https://github.com/vercel/next.js/issues/6040, this treats each module as a separate build context.

I also considered using [Turborepo](https://turborepo.org/), but this also treats each module as a separate Next.js application.

title: How to register a callback to run after server response is sent?
body:
My application accesses a database within a transaction so I need to commit/rollback the transaction and return the database connect to the pool after request processing is complete.

This comment here https://github.com/vercel/next.js/discussions/29750#discussioncomment-1573204 suggests using `Response.waitUntil()` as a substitute for `res.on('finish', callback)` but I don't believe that can work since waitUntil is supplied a promise (which would start executing immediately).

I think I can also rule out NextResponse.next() since it does not return the final response, just a sentinel for the middleware executor.

author: prate3k

title: Server Components use case question, compared to getStaticProps
body:
I'm building a website that fetches data from a fairly complicated Sanity dataset. One of the more complicated parts is rich text - it may have an `internalLink` annotating a piece of text, or it may have an accordion that has a callout with body text with an `internalLink`, etc. The references within these `internalLink` annotations need to be expanded so I can determine its proper URL. Sometimes, I need to then get rich text *within* one of these referenced documents.

When using `getStaticProps`, I need to write a very deeply nested query to Sanity so I can fetch all of the data I need for my view. So, all of my data fetching is on the "page" or view level.

One of the things I like most about frameworks like Apollo is that I can define data requirements and handle fetching on the component level - so I'm very interested in leveraging server components to do the same. This would allow my page-level query to be very simple, then, my `InternalLink` react component can fetch the referenced doc on its own whenever it is rendered.

A crucial part of the website is that the full view is pre-rendered. 

If I were to begin using server components, would I lose out on any pre-rendering benefits? Would next be able to serve a "complete" page with all of these `internalLink`s fully resolved? Or, would these server components end up fetching for each user?

title: Rewrites are only configurable at build time
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

Operating System:
      Platform: darwin
      Arch: x64
      Version: Darwin Kernel Version 20.4.0: Fri Mar  5 01:14:14 PST 2021; root:xnu-7195.101.1~3/RELEASE_X86_64
    Binaries:
      Node: 16.13.2
      npm: 8.1.2
      Yarn: 1.22.17
      pnpm: 6.29.1
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.0.10
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.10

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.13.2

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### How are you deploying your application?

next start

### Describe the Bug

`rewrites()` in `next.config.js` is only called at build time.
IMHO it should be called at run time.

*Use Case:* If I don't know beforehand the url to where I want to rewrite I can't define it at runtime from an Environment variable.

### Expected Behavior

`rewrites()` should be called at runtime so that we can have a dynamic rewrite url using an env variable.

### To Reproduce

put a log in `rewrites()` in `next.config.js` and verify that it is only called at build time and not at runtime.

title: HowTo CORS, Trusted Hosts and remote API
body:
Hi,

For the past few days I've been trying to figure out how can I send requests to a remote API service on the client side. Whenever I send a request from the client I get the following error in the browser: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/CORS/Errors/CORSMultipleAllowOriginNotAllowed. 

Since NextJS is running in development mode on a Docker container running NodeJS I am assuming that the headers are set by internal web server which Next.js uses. How can I modify these headers to allow CORS on certain hosts?

I am aware that Next.JS has it's own API which can be used to act as a middle man but I want to avoid this and instead send requests directly to the remote API from the client side.

To get some idea what I am trying to achieve imagine the following. I have a Docker stack which has a container running NextJS in development mode and I have another service which represents the remote API. They are on the same Docker network. And both of them are available publicly using dedicated domains. For the code which is running on NextJS serverside part I am accessing the service using http://internalServiceName and for the code which runs on the NextJS clientside I am accessing the service using the public url of that service. The code which runs server side performs as expected without issues however the client side code runs into CORS issue while accessing the remote service.

Since I do not know how to modify the headers on the web server which is powered when I run "next dev" I tried to solve my issue using proxy with ['async rewrites'](https://nextjs.org/docs/api-reference/next.config.js/rewrites).

```
  async rewrites() {
    return [
      {
        source: '/external-api/:path',
        destination:  'http://myapi.somedomain.com'+ '/:path',
      },
    ]
  },
```

Basically on the code which is executed on the client side I would use /external-api/myroute and I expected this to get rewritten to destination. However when using this method I am getting 500 error from NextJS web server with the following error in the console:  `error - Error: connect ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:80`.

I am currently without ideas, so any advice is welcome.
author: jelovac

title: Slow Load/Connecting Time for Network Requests
body:
Hello,

Referencing #17977, I seem to have a similar problem with load times, but in reverse. I'm having the problem in Firefox, but in Chrome everything loads incredibly fast.

[One user suggested](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/discussions/17977#discussioncomment-991286) to "Clear Site Data" on chrome, which seems to have helped a lot of users with no negative feedback. However, I can't seem to do exactly the same on Firefox. Even a private browser window in Firefox is still slow.

So as an example, I will load "Series" (tv shows/movies) from a local API with a local DB. This request will stay "Connecting" for exactly 2 seconds.

Initial page load:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7909209/152415651-6a0865c9-12df-43b6-a387-e1e10ac1c36a.png)

Loading Series:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7909209/152413428-0511c0c6-3aae-4bd4-bf8d-8d63467ccee7.png)

Then, when that is done, it renders cards. On the card is a URL for an image through the same API. That then takes over 2 seconds. 

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7909209/152413558-a855dc15-7871-4862-b433-3cad43e1da68.png)

So in total, from load to finish, it takes over 6 seconds. This is despite both the "Series" data request and images being cached in the browser.

If I do the same thing on Chrome (which I wasn't using for development), it is significantly faster. For example, the same thing with cached data:

Initial page load:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7909209/152415824-51b13912-7554-48b0-8479-5ec42871a936.png)

Series data request:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7909209/152413826-7510af78-7cb6-4ab4-b20f-3e5885199911.png)

Image requests:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7909209/152413870-f79bab4e-9962-4899-9029-a1060078e860.png)

---

title: revalidating ssr page caches with renderToHTML failed after nextjs v12
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

    Operating System:
      Platform: linux
      Arch: x64
      Version: #29~20.04.1-Ubuntu SMP Fri Jan 14 00:32:30 UTC 2022
    Binaries:
      Node: 14.18.2
      npm: 6.14.15
      Yarn: 1.22.15
      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.0.9
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.9

### What version of Node.js are you using?

14.18.2

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

Ubuntu 20

### How are you deploying your application?

custom server with express.js

### Describe the Bug

We cached SSR pages in a redis database. after each hit on cache we want to revalidate the cache value in redis.
before migrating to next v12 every thing was ok. see the following code for revalidating cache:

``` js
async function revalidateCache(key, req) {
  try {
    const res = {}
    const value = await app.renderToHTML(
      { ...req, is_revalidate: true },
      res,
      '/browse',
      { ...getParamsFromUrl(req.url), ...req.query }
    )
    memcached_client.set(key, value)
  } catch (error) {
    console.log('revalidation error: ', error)
  }
}
```
which caused the following error:
`TypeError: Cannot read property 'Symbol(NextRequestMeta)' of undefined
    at Object.getRequestMeta (/home/torob/Desktop/Repositories/nextjs/node_modules/next/dist/server/request-meta.js:12:21)
    at Object.renderToHTML (/home/torob/Desktop/Repositories/nextjs/node_modules/next/dist/server/render.js:342:153)
    at async doRender (/home/torob/Desktop/Repositories/nextjs/node_modules/next/dist/server/base-server.js:855:38)
    at async /home/torob/Desktop/Repositories/nextjs/node_modules/next/dist/server/base-server.js:950:28
    at async /home/torob/Desktop/Repositories/nextjs/node_modules/next/dist/server/response-cache.js:63:36`

``` js

after removing is_validate as follow:

async function revalidateCache(key, req) {
  try {
    const res = {}
    const value = await app.renderToHTML(
      req,
      res,
      '/browse',
      { ...getParamsFromUrl(req.url), ...req.query }
    )
    memcached_client.set(key, value)
  } catch (error) {
    console.log('revalidation error: ', error)
  }
}
```

error changed to this:
`TypeError: Cannot read property 'finished' of undefined
    at Object.isResSent (/home/torob/Desktop/Repositories/nextjs/node_modules/next/dist/shared/lib/utils.js:40:16)
    at Object.renderToHTML (/home/torob/Desktop/Repositories/nextjs/node_modules/next/dist/server/render.js:660:21)
    at async doRender (/home/torob/Desktop/Repositories/nextjs/node_modules/next/dist/server/base-server.js:855:38)
    at async /home/torob/Desktop/Repositories/nextjs/node_modules/next/dist/server/base-server.js:950:28
    at async /home/torob/Desktop/Repositories/nextjs/node_modules/next/dist/server/response-cache.js:63:36`

it seems that renderToHTML needs real req and res and after res.end we could not get html to update the cache.

### Expected Behavior

I need a api like renderToHTML to get html from nextjs.

### To Reproduce

create a simple express custom server and for a route (for example /a) send a fake response as follow:

`res.send('hello world')`

after that try to get real html from nextjs with renderToHTML or render.

title: Pass set-cookie header from fetch inside getServerSideProps
body:
Hi! 

I'm new to NextJS and I have a question about `getServerSideProps`.

I would like to,
1. From within `getServerSideProps` make a `fetch` request to my backend (rails).
2. Get the `Set-cookie` header in the response from that `fetch` request 
3. Add the `Set-cookie` header to the `ctx.response` object i get from the `getServerSideProps` props. 

I have the following code,
```javascript
export const getServerSideProps: GetServerSideProps = async ({ res }) => {
    const fetchResponse = await fetch('someUrl',{method:post, credentials: 'same-origin'})
    res.setHeader('set-cookie', fetchResponse.headers.get('set-cookie'))
}
```

But I always get `null` back from`fetchResponse.headers.get('set-cookie')`. 
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5450557/152511935-a4ba47de-34c1-4e5d-bfc3-260244ddec89.png)

I have tried the `fetch` request from code in a `useEffect` inside a NextJS page and that returns the correct header and the browser sets the cookie.

Is this at all possible to do?

**What I've read**
Browser implementations of [fetch](https://fetch.spec.whatwg.org/#forbidden-response-header-name) does not allow reading the `Set-cookie` header from client side code. But from what I can see NextJS uses a [polyfill](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/canary/packages/next/server/node-polyfill-fetch.js) which builds on `node-fetch`. According to their [tests](https://github.com/node-fetch/node-fetch/blob/44c67b11a36f83d2c9e0cf1671043f2a87c058f9/test/test.js#L1212-L1231) they have support to read this header.

title: Running background jobs
body:
Is there any serverless solution for scheduling background jobs? I need to track a 3rd party transaction/trade and check if it is completed within 5 minutes. I came up with other ideas, but none of them seem as good as background jobs.

I have thought about making the client periodically call a serverless function that checks if the transaction/trade was completed, but it's a very unreliable approach. If the client closes the browser tab or gets disconnected, the tracking stops, so that's not a good idea.

Another approach would be to make a recursive serverless function. I'm not sure if this would work and it seems like a bad idea.

Is there any other solution or should I make a separate server that is responsible for tracking 3rd party transactions/trades and updating the database accordingly?

title: Dynamic imports are not prefetched
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

```
/bin/sh: yarn: command not found
/bin/sh: pnpm: command not found

    Operating System:
      Platform: darwin
      Arch: x64
      Version: Darwin Kernel Version 19.6.0: Sun Nov 14 19:58:51 PST 2021; root:xnu-6153.141.50~1/RELEASE_X86_64
    Binaries:
      Node: 14.18.1
      npm: 6.14.15
      Yarn: N/A
      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.0.11-canary.4
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2
```

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.11-canary.4

### What version of Node.js are you using?

14.18.1

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### How are you deploying your application?

next start

### Describe the Bug

(See the code here: https://github.com/dwbruhn/nextjs-dynamic)

When a component is dynamically imported (using `next/dynamic`):

```js
import dynamic from 'next/dynamic';
const DynamicEmailComponent = dynamic(() => import('../components/Email'))
```

...and configured to be omitted from the render until some client side state change:

```js
{checked && <DynamicEmailComponent />}
```

...the javascript chunk for that component is not loaded until the actual state change.

In the gif below, the `638.87d211e4fb0fac8e.js` file is not requested until the user clicks the checkbox for the first time:

![dynamic-no-prefetch](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8230981/152580288-056315cc-54d0-4f35-bdb9-d95f44b39cda.gif)

### Expected Behavior

I would have expected the initial page html to include a `<link rel="prefetch" ...>` tag to prefetch the js chunk for the dynamic component. In that case, when the user clicks the checkbox, the chunk would already have been loaded (if the browser decided to honor the prefetch request).

(Since Next.js prefetches links to pages, I would expect it to also prefetch dynamic imports within a single page, which is why I decided to file this as a bug. But if it's actually a feature request, feel free to categorize it!)

### To Reproduce

Install and run the app here in production mode, then click on the checkbox:

https://github.com/dwbruhn/nextjs-dynamic

title: Switch between getStaticProps/Paths and getServerSideProps based on a condition.
body:
Well, i'm using NextJS to build a blog and generating the pages statically is the way to go, but as said on the docs, "In development (next dev), getStaticPaths will be called on every request.", and it is very annoying. Every time i need to go to a post i need to wait almost 2 seconds and this isn't very developer friendly, but using getServerSideProps i can have a perfect DX, as it can be fetched before visiting the page using next/Link.

So i was wondering, is it possible to change to getStaticProps ONLY when in production? Like:
`export process.env.NODE_ENV == "production" ? { getStaticPaths, getStaticProps } : { getServerSideProps };`

title: Pre-fetching Data on the Client-side
body:
Sorry for the long essay folks.

In Nuxt.js, there are two functions for pre-fetching data before a page component is mounted - `asyncData` and `fetch`.

Both of functions work on the client-side - `fetch` doesn't run on the server; `asyncData` can run on the server.

Currently, all the exported data fetching functions in Next.js only work on the server-side, or requires you to refresh the page. Client-side navigation doesn't invoke those functions.

In Next.js, Is there a way to load data and populate them in a page component's `props` before the page is rendered, like Nuxt's `asyncData` and `fetch`?

I don't want to show the skeleton UIs and spinners.

author: domvo

title: Static export without clientside JavaScript
body:
### Describe the feature you'd like to request

For certain projects, I want the lightness of a pure HTML and CSS static website build, with no clientside JS. These projects can have multiple pages that use shared components (so Next is still a good fit) but the components are all pure and simple so there's no need for clientside hydration.

It's already possible to extend `Document` and omit the `<NextScript />` tag when rendering.  But this has a shortcoming: clientside JS chunks are still included in the build output, and more importantly, they are still downloaded to the client because of `<link rel="preload">` tags rendered by Next's `<Head />` component, wasting bandwidth.

### Describe the solution you'd like

A flag on `next build` (or an alternative command) that tells Next not to generate any clientside script bundles/chunks at all during build. `<NextScript />` would therefore render nothing, and `<Head />` wouldn't render any preload links.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

It looks like it's [possible](https://www.tezify.com/how-to/avoid-preloading-scripts-with-nextjs/) to create your own alternative `Head` component that omits the preload links, and I've tried this, but it feels a bit brittle/unsupported, and clientside chunks are still built unnecessarily. Ideally I'd like a formal way to say "no clientside JS please".
author: callumlocke

title: Option to specify chroma subsampling for images
body: ### Describe the feature you'd like to request This PR: https://github.com/vercel/next.js/pull/31494 made AVIF images smaller (in size) by default which is a **great** thing. However in certain cases, the quality of the image is degraded and it seems that the [quality](https://nextjs.org/docs/api-reference/next/image#quality) prop does not help. After some investigation, the root cause seems to be the **chroma subsampling** rather than the quality. --------- I created this repository for an easier reproduction: https://github.com/thibautsabot/sharp-chroma. In the [main script ](https://github.com/thibautsabot/sharp-chroma/blob/main/index.js) I tried to roughly re-create what transformations `next/image` is doing. By keeping the default chroma subsampling to `4:2:0` the image looks like this: But by using `4:4:4` (a value from the [sharp documentation](https://sharp.pixelplumbing.com/api-output#jpeg)) the image looks much nicer: ### Describe the solution you'd like I understand that `4:4:4` should not be the default option since the output is fine for most images and that the size is about 5-10% bigger (according to some very quick tests that I made). For this reason, I'm rather suggesting the ability to pass options directly to sharp / squoosh, to have better control on the transformers. That way, we could optimise further on a per-image basis. _(If you like the idea, I can try to make a PR for that)._ ### Describe alternatives you've considered We could use the [unoptimzed](https://nextjs.org/docs/api-reference/next/image#unoptimized) prop to use the image as-is, and it'd work fine, but we'd lose the benefit of the Image component. That being said, I don't know how often this "issue" occurs and I am absolutely not an expert in image compression. Maybe it's only on very small images (like the one in the example) and not optimising them further wouldn't be a problem. But perhaps it's a more common problem and we could benefit from additional options. Would love your input on this 🙂 !

title: webpack cache does not update when node_modules change, even after server restart
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

NA

### What version of Next.js are you using?

11.1.3

### What version of Node.js are you using?

v12.22.8

### What browser are you using?

Chrome

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### How are you deploying your application?

own setup with AWS

### Describe the Bug

During development, we symlink to some local dependencies and install them in our nextjs project. These show up in node modules. When any file in these dependencies change, we expect that next js would hot reload the changes, instead what we have observed is that the webpack build runs again, but the changes are not reflected on the UI. 

On debugging more, we could figure out that although the files in node_modules have changed, the files loaded by webpack on the browser are the same and not the new changed ones. We tried restarting the server to see if it fixes this, that does not seem to work.  Since node modules is a special folder, we figured that it is possible that there are some specific rules for node modules due to which the changes are not being watched or something, so we also tried making some other changes in the app code so that hot reload happens and it can maybe pickup the changes in node modules as well. This also does not work.

On further understanding, it seems like the filesystem cache created by nextjs webpack config, is not updating even though the symlinked dependency has updated in node modules. If we delete the cache and run the server again, it works alright.


This was working fine before nextjs 11, and webpack 5

### Expected Behavior

We would expect the changes in node modules to be hot reloaded and included in the build 

### To Reproduce

- add a custom webpack config
- add config.resolve.symlinks = false in your custom config
- in package json refer to a package from your local filesystem (npm will treat it as a symlinked package)
- npm i to install the local package
- consume the local package in your app
- start the server
- things work as expected
- make some changes in the local package
- these changes are not reflected / not hot reloaded 
- try restarting the server - still do not get reflected
- try making other code changes in the app, the other code changes get reflected, but not the ones in local package
- try deleting the node modules , package lock, and do an npm install again  - things work fine, changes are available
- OR try deleting the .next/cache , restart the server - things work fine, changes are available
- make some changes in the webpack config, restart the server -  this sometimes leads to cache invalidation, things work fine, changes are available

title: Built-in CSS support is disabled with no custom CSS configuration
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

_No response_

### What version of Next.js are you using?

10.2.3

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.13.1

### What browser are you using?

Firefox

### What operating system are you using?

macOS

### How are you deploying your application?

next export

### Describe the Bug

I have custom webpack rules have `test` and `include` both.
If there is a rule have `test` and `include`, [Webpack loads only files match `test` and `include` both](https://webpack.js.org/configuration/module/#rule-conditions).
But, I saw [code](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/canary/packages/next/build/webpack-config.ts#L1900) that Next.js disable built-in CSS support, when either `test` **or** `include` matches.
So there can be a case disabling built-in CSS support without custom CSS configuration.

### Expected Behavior

Built-in CSS support is not disabled.

### To Reproduce

Add custom webpack configuration rule that have `test` and `include` both:

```js
{
  test: /\.js$/,
  include: () => false,
  // ...
}
```

and then execute `next dev` in your terminal, you can see warning built-in CSS support is disabled.

I thought about:

title: Multisite / multi website support
body:
### Describe the feature you'd like to request

I would like to have more out of the box multi website support. For example if you have several websites that share a lot of similar  functionality, e.g.:

localhost:3000/website/ => `www.website.com`
localhost:3000/other/ => `www.otherwebsite.com`

### Describe the solution you'd like

Not entirely sure, to be able to configure this simply by config files and perhaps folder structure in /pages/ ?

I guess any solution would be good but as few steps and simple as possible :) Would need to be able to do:

- Localisation per website - e.g. `localhost:3000/website1/[lang]/`
- Be able to have different global styles per website, preferably one super global stylesheet for all and then support for one for each website.  

I am still investigating a bit what is possible today and there is probably more things that I've missed.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

You can sort of already do this with some semi-major downsides:

- You need to implement localisation manually because the standard solution becomes `localhost:3000/en/website1/` instead of `localhost:3000/website1/en/` and then you cannot have all the languages of that website live on the same domain unless you use subdomains which you probably don't want either.
- Localisation per website, one website might only have 1 language while another has 3
- Global styles affect all websites, there is no built in possibility of adding global styles to only one website (without it having to be downloaded at least for the other websites)

title: ISR: Diff between previously cached and new pages in getStaticProps
body:
👋 
I'm currently working on server-side error handling for our application.
For most pages with ISR - we used to send `{ notFound: true }` with small revalidation time in case of any mission-critical API errors (due to backend instability under load - so it's safe to assume that issue is temporary and is going to be resolved automatically in a short amount of time). Now I'm slowly migrating to raising errors instead since it would cause revalidation process to fail -> previously cached version would be served to users. 

It is preferred, _but with one exception_ - if the page was never built in the first place - it would result in 500 page. I'm switching to serve 500 instead of 404, seems not ideal.

This raises one question/idea - would it be possible to somehow distinguish between previously cached and new page inside `getStaticProps` - to handle errors in different way. (Or I'm totally missing something else here)
author: harvestnide

title: Router cannot refresh page if url has anchor
body:
### Describe the feature you'd like to request

I need to refresh current page with calling `getServerSideProps`, but without reloading whole page (`router.reload`, `window.location.reload`). 

Router has no special method for that, so I using:
```js
this.props.router.replace(this.props.router.asPath, undefined, {
    scroll: false
})
```

But if current page url has `#anchor`, reload do not works.


### Describe the solution you'd like

Some option to "force" push/replace.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

Current workaround is:
1. Preserve the anchor hash - it will be removed:
```js
const asPath = this.props.router.asPath
const [ path, hash ] = asPath.split('#')
```
2. Go to path without anchor hash:
```js
const reloadOptions = {
    scroll: false
}
this.props.router.replace(path, undefined, reloadOptions)
```
3. And reload it **again!**
```js
if (hash) {
    this.props.router.replace(path, undefined, reloadOptions)
    ...
}
```
4. Restore the hash:
```js
    window.location.hash = '#' + hash;
```

2) then, in `MyApp` (the component of `_app.js`) put each map into a `useState` and transfer the `state` and the `setState` functions in a react `Context` (i'm aware that maps are not a good fit with useState but i'm still prototyping and not sure if i want to use immutable js or something)
3) the maps would then be shared between multiple pages like i want them to

but the client never received the `Map`s i created in the `getInitalProps` function and so the `<details>` elements the server rendered were not rendered on the client and so this mismatch happened.

When i turned the maps into regular objects it worked fine, i originally wanted them to be converted into maps straight in `MyApp.getInitialProps`, but i can just transform them into maps in the `App` too.

If it's possible i would love it if warnings or errors for that would be added but I'm not sure, this is probably just me not reading docs

title: Firefox extension content script not installed after client-side page change
body:
### Run `next info` (available from version 12.0.8 and up)

```
/bin/sh: 1: pnpm: not found

    Operating System:
      Platform: linux
      Arch: x64
      Version: #202112220937~1640185481~20.04~b3a2c21-Ubuntu SMP Mon Jan 3 17:0
    Binaries:
      Node: 16.13.1
      npm: 8.1.2
      Yarn: 3.1.0
      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:
      next: 12.0.9
      react: 17.0.2
      react-dom: 17.0.2
```

### What version of Next.js are you using?

12.0.9

### What version of Node.js are you using?

16.13.1

### What browser are you using?

Firefox

### What operating system are you using?

Pop_OS! 20.04 (Ubuntu 20.04)

### How are you deploying your application?

Vercel

### Describe the Bug

My [app](https://github.com/t1v1/thavma) includes a Firefox extension and communicates with said extension (to ensure that it's installed) via the `postMessage` API to a `content_script` installed on `thavma.io/assessments` and `localhost/assessments`:

```json
// ext/manifest.json
  "content_scripts": [
    {
      "matches": [
        "http://localhost/assessments",
        "http://localhost/assessments?*",
        "https://thavma.io/assessments",
        "https://thavma.io/assessments?*"
      ],
      "js": ["thavma.js"],
      "all_frames": true
    }
  ],
```

Based on that `manifest.json`, Firefox loads the `thavma.js` extension script whenever the URL is `/assessments`. This works perfectly fine when one types in the entire URL (i.e. `localhost:3000/assessments` or `thavma.io/assessments`) but when the user visits the home page (i.e. `localhost:3000/`) and then uses the navigation header to perform a client-side page change to `/assessments`, the `content_script` is not installed and thus isn't detected by my app.

### Expected Behavior

The way that Next.js performs client-side page transitions should notify Firefox (and Chromium-based browsers too, of course) to install any registered extension scripts to that URL.

### To Reproduce

You should be able to pretty easily reproduce with the original [`thavma`](https://github.com/t1v1/thavma) repository:
1. Clone the repository: `git clone https://github.com/t1v1/thavma`
2. Install dependencies: `cd thavma && yarn install`
3. Start the Next.js development server: `yarn dev`
4. Open Firefox, go to `about:debugging`, click "This Firefox", click "Load Temporary Add-on...", and select the `thavma/ext/manifest.json` file.
5. Open up the console and go to `localhost:3000/assessments` (if you hit a paywall, use the Stripe test card `4242 4242 4242 4242` and any CVC, postal code, etc).
6. See the logs from the `ext/thavma.js` script:

![extension-logs](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/93789775/153329219-6a3222bb-1b14-4304-8e33-6079db8625ed.png)

7. Now, go to `localhost:3000/` and click "assessments" in the header to perform a client-side page change to `/assessments`.
8. Open up the console and notice that there are no logs from the `ext/thavma.js` script... it wasn't loaded by Firefox as expected.

If you're somehow unable to reproduce using those steps, or you really want something simpler, I can probably take a couple hours to create a smaller repro for you. I really think this bug shouldn't be overlooked in a framework as polished as Next.

title: Surface Webpack build errors
body:
### Describe the feature you'd like to request

I've seen several cases where `next build` fails with obscure webpack errors. E.g.

```
Failed to compile.

webpack/runtime/compat
The "path" argument must be of type string. Received undefined


> Build failed because of webpack errors
```
and
```
Failed to compile.

HookWebpackError: Maximum call stack size exceeded


> Build error occurred
Error: > Build failed because of webpack errors
    at <root>/node_modules/next/dist/build/index.js:397:19
    at async Span.traceAsyncFn (<root>/universe/node_modules/next/dist/telemetry/trace/trace.js:60:20)
    at async Object.build [as default] (<root>/universe/node_modules/next/dist/build/index.js:77:25)
```


The causes were traced to different things (the former involves some dynamic import and the latter is bad huge sourcemap from another library), but the point is that it's nearly impossible to understand where the error is coming from without commenting lines in my app/step through next's build process.

### Describe the solution you'd like

Surface from Webpack what modules are involved with the error.
In the case of the first error mentioned, a stack trace would be helpful.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

Debug and step through Next's build.

So I have these beautiful hero-images at the top of each page fixed positioned. Part of it is hidden by menu and content. While scrolling up, the user "explores" all of it. In order that this all works nicely at different screen sizeses / mobile I need different aspect ratios.

So for mobile I use an aspect ratio of 4:3, on large screen it is rather 2/1. So far I prepared different explicit images for that pupose and displayed them with img srcset.

It would be nice toto automatize that and simply provide a large 4.3 image and have next/image do the magic. In order for that to happen, I would need to write somewhat a custom loader. Does something like that already exist? Any ideas how to approach that?

body:
I have a bunch of `.md` files I need to expose for the web app I'm working on and other apps. I have one endpoint `/pages/api/v1/get/[id].ts` that parses and returns a single file with a given id, and also an endpoint `/pages/api/v0/legacy.ts` that reads all files and turns them into XML file. Since all data is known at build time it'd be convenient if the api routes could be statically generated to save time for each request as well as compute time.

title: 💾💧 Memory leak
body: Hi all 👋🏻, Strap in because this is a long one... 👨‍💻 ## Introduction To start I want to express I am looking to get some opinions & help from people more experienced with memory issues in NodeJS / NextJS. I also want to mention I am not an expert at memory issues the following message expresses my findings and questions on the topic. ### Specifications NextJS: v12.0.10 Local Node: v16.14.0 Docker Node: v16.13.2 PNPM: v6.28.0 ## What's the issue? We host a NextJS application on kubernetes we started noticing that the pods would crash and produce the following in the logs: ``` <--- Last few GCs ---> [261:0x7f3a2a4bf130] 99059389 ms: Mark-sweep (reduce) 2006.5 (2052.8) -> 2006.5 (2052.3) MB, 3771.9 / 6.5 ms (average mu = 0.918, current mu = 0.000) last resort GC in old space requested [261:0x7f3a2a4bf130] 99063314 ms: Mark-sweep (reduce) 2006.5 (2052.3) -> 2006.5 (2052.3) MB, 3925.0 / 8.4 ms (average mu = 0.846, current mu = 0.000) last resort GC in old space requested <--- JS stacktrace ---> FATAL ERROR: CALL_AND_RETRY_LAST Allocation failed - JavaScript heap out of memory ``` Once the pods had restarted we monitored the memory and noticed that over the space of roughly a day each pod would progressively increase in memory from 200MB before crashing at the limit set by kubernetes (4GB). ### Production: ## Application Background The application is very simple it sits behind authentication and its sole purpose is to show information cards to a user so that they can be navigated to other team's applications. The cards are pulled down using an api request from the client to `/api/cards` from here it makes 4 requests out to other apis in order to get the card information from various teams this data is then concatenated together then sent back to the client. The application also handles the ability to change users personal data email, name, phone number ect. The site averages 20,000 - 28,000 users a day. ## What have you tried I haven't necessarily used memory inspection tools in great detail before so this was new for me.. First thing we tried was to see if we could replicate the leak locally At first glance (No traffic) dev tools is reporting the in use heap size at 33.1MB. In the activity monitor one of the two node processes is reported to be at 274.2MB in memory the other at 66.4MB. On first visit of the site (Homepage only) the memory on the mac activity monitor increases from 274.2MB to 288.6MB (+14.4MB) the in use heap size is 33.1MB On the second visit of the node process in the activity monitor goes up again to 297.9 (+9.3MB) however the heap size stays the same at 33.1MB. On third visit it increases to 312MB (+14.1MB) Heap still at 33.1MB. I left some time between each visit to give the memory a chance to settle before taking note of the value. The memory never goes down between changes it always increases. 📈 When running `process.memoryUsage()` locally I get the following: ```js {rss: 101584896, heapTotal: 35713024, heapUsed: 33706512, external: 1348446, arrayBuffers: 283369} ``` Why is the RSS so small compared to the process in activity monitor? Because the heap stays the same size throughout the test I guess doing a heap snapshot won't yield much in the way of useful results? (I am not 100% sure on this so please tell me if I am wrong). I did one anyways I can't share it all but let me know if you want me to drill down further into any of these: I did find [this article](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/discussions/26801#discussioncomment-1091592) but adding: `experimental: { isrMemoryCacheSize: 0 },` didn't help. I then moved on from this and tried using kubectl to inspect the node process of our staging environment. I managed to get at the console and type a few commands: ```js // process.memoryUsage(); {rss: 165564416, heapTotal: 71540736, heapUsed: 66266672, external: 3182269, arrayBuffers: 4619657} {rss: 172294144, heapTotal: 73056256, heapUsed: 68522432, external: 1903979, arrayBuffers: 4849625} // After 5 mins {rss: 172605440, heapTotal: 73056256, heapUsed: 69137200, external: 1903995, arrayBuffers: 4849641} // immediately after the previous one {rss: 178196480, heapTotal: 72851456, heapUsed: 66142240, external: 3290508, arrayBuffers: 4475957} // After first view ``` ```js // v8.getHeapStatistics() { does_zap_garbage: 0 heap_size_limit: 1660944384 malloced_memory: 1065032 number_of_detached_contexts: 0 number_of_native_contexts: 1 peak_malloced_memory: 17259720 total_available_size: 1593436032 total_heap_size: 71540736 total_heap_size_executable: 3145728 total_physical_size: 70831656 used_heap_size: 67235784 } ``` Staging Pods: With each visit of the staging site the memory increases. Been banging my head against a wall on this one for sure. Please let me know what you would like me to try to find the source of the leak. 🙏

title: Docs: How UI Libraries should handle detecting touch devices while supporting SSR?
body:
### What is the improvement or update you wish to see?

Hey team, just had a quick question related to building a [UI library](https://tamagui.dev) with Next SSR support...

I have a few components that want to render things differently for touch devices only - stuff like "hover on desktop, but tap (or null) on touch". In some cases you'd want to diverge even more than that. But if I detect touch device on the browser, on server it will differ and cause SSR hydration mismatch issues.

Somewhat relevant SO here: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/59494037/how-to-detect-the-device-on-react-ssr-app-with-next-js

The library doesn't depend on Next at all, but it needs some way to either:

- Render always as non-touch always on server, then on hydration swap things out without causing hydration mismatch errors (as it does now). Is it possible to indicate "this component will change on hydration, and that's ok"?
- Alternatively or alongside, the library could expose `setTouchDevice` and then indicate that with Next.js you need to have an extra step of setup (where it parses the UA to determine touch device and then calls `setTouchDevice` before it begins the render).

Docs didn't have a ton of info here, so wanted to open this to both get clarity and maybe add something about this to docs.

### Is there any context that might help us understand?

---

### Does the docs page already exist? Please link to it.

_No response_

title: NX Monorepo Next.js project deployment via Gitlab CI to Vercel
body:
I am trying do deploy a NX Monorepo Next.js project via Gitlab CI to Vercel. Here is `.yml` file .
```
image: node:lts
stages:
  - build
  - web-test
  - deploy
cache:
  key: ${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}
  paths:
    - node_modules/
build website:
  stage: build
  script:
    - npm install
    - npm i @nrwl/cli -g
    - nx run shop-wine:build
  artifacts:
    paths:
      - ./dist
test website:
  stage: web-test
  script:
    - npm install
    - npm i @nrwl/cli -g
    - nx run shop-wine:serve &
    - sleep 60
    - curl "http://localhost:4200" | grep -q "Welcome shop-wine"
deploy-to-vercel:
  stage: deploy

  variables:
    PREVIEW_URL: $CI_PROJECT_NAME-$CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG.$VERCEL_USER.vercel.app
    VERCEL_ORG_ID: $VERCEL_ORG_ID
    VERCEL_PROJECT_ID: $VERCEL_PROJECT_ID

  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_TAG
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == 'main'

  environment:
    name: vercel/$CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME
    url: https://$PREVIEW_URL

  script:
    - npm i -g vercel
    - DEPLOYMENT_URL=$(VERCEL_ORG_ID=$VERCEL_ORG_ID VERCEL_PROJECT_ID=$VERCEL_PROJECT_ID vercel --confirm -t $VERCEL_TOKEN --scope $VERCEL_USER)
    - vercel alias set $DEPLOYMENT_URL $PREVIEW_URL -t $VERCEL_TOKEN --scope $VERCEL_USER
```
There are no problems with `build `and `web-test` stages but when I get to manual `deploy-to-vercel` stage I am getting the following error from GitLab:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/23064521/153524853-e654f497-86fc-4a20-8987-06a23839d208.png)

Here are the overrides on Vercel:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/23064521/153524924-f99a7cf9-ea9e-46b5-a50c-db554d127a95.png)

I am also using a plugin to automaticaly deploy to Vercel. As you can see it is deployed with success ahead of `build`, `web-test` and manual `deploy-to-vercel` stages.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/23064521/153525000-87f976fa-1e1f-4d82-961a-131fd97f47cf.png)

But when it gets to manual `deploy-to-vercel` here is Vercel error. Let me know if I am doing something wrong.

author: khg0712

title: Redirect requests with defaultLocale
body:
## What I want to achieve

Assume I have the following `i18n` config:
```
i18n: {
  locales: ['en', 'fr'],
  defaultLocale: 'en',
},
```

I want to 301 redirect any request with defaultLocale (`en`) in its url to not having the locale in its url. For example, when users open `localhost/en/something`, they are redirected to `localhost/something`. On the other hand, if users open `localhost/fr/something` (this is not the default locale), the redirection shouldn't happen.

Reason of doing this is that we have good SEO ranking on those `localhost/something` pages, so we don't want to disrupt those ranking by introducing `localhost/en/something`, which essentially serving the same content.

Another note: we are using SSG for those pages.

## What I have tried
- Tried `rewrites` but certainly it is not redirecting
- Tried `redirect`, but showed `Redirect too many times` error
- Tried to use middleware, but I am unable to detect if user is opening `/en/something` or `/something`

I'm still new to NextJS, so any guidance will be very much appreciated! 🙏

title: Will v13 drop IE11 support?
body: IE adds big drag to final bundles: - bigger JS because of unsupported syntax transformed by babel - crazy HTML and JS boilerplate around `` tag because of unsupported width/height aspect ratios and `loading="lazy"` Microsoft stopped IE11 support for [Teams and 365 already](https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/microsoft-365-blog/microsoft-365-apps-say-farewell-to-internet-explorer-11-and/ba-p/1591666) and will event [officially phase out support](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/lifecycle/faq/internet-explorer-microsoft-edge#what-is-the-lifecycle-policy-for-internet-explorer-) for the browser itself June 15th, 2022. Is Next.js team planning to stop supporting IE11 in next major release (whenever that be)?

title: How to execute long-running background functions (tasks) in production?
body:
I have an app where after user signs in with 3rd party application, user's followings are retrieved from that service.
For each of the followings, I get more data from another API, and store this overall information in my database.

In my test account with 70 followings, this process takes ~20 minutes (includes timeouts due to API rate limits).
```js
const addFollowings = async followings => {
	const followingsData = []
	for (const following of followings) {
		const { id, name } = following
		const personInfo = new PersonInfo(name)
		await personInfo.init()
		const number = ...
                ...
		followingsData.push({ name, id, number, ... })

                console.log('new following:', name)
	}
	await insertFollowingsIntoDB(followingsData)
}
```
This works fine in development. But I have just deployed the app on Vercel, and when it comes to executing this function, firstly only a few followings are console-logged, then the function gets stuck: nothing is logged. Only when I refresh the page (which triggers `/api/auth/session` serverless function to get called), a few more are logged.

I guess this is because Vercel 10s limit on execution for functions.
Please advice how I should handle this. Thanks in advance.
